action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   12345678
  +--------+
 1|..#..#..|
 2|..#.....|
 3|......#A|
 4|2#...p.x|
 5|1.......|
 6|..y...S.|
 7|....#...|
 8|#...q...|
  +--------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (7,6) at t=0, FACING N.
- You always have a facing direction (N, S, E or W). Each tick you make exactly one move:
  F    = step one cell forward, in the direction you are facing
  L    = turn 90 degrees left, in place (you do not move, but the tick is consumed)
  R    = turn 90 degrees right, in place (you do not move, but the tick is consumed)
  WAIT = do nothing for one tick
  There are no absolute moves: to go "north" you must first be facing N, then move F.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving F into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are (facing unchanged) and
     the tick is wasted. L, R and WAIT never bump.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. This applies even on
     ticks where you only turned or waited. Passing a guard mid-move is safe; only
     ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (7,6) facing N again, the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the
  cancelled moves' ticks are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (8,3): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (6,4); t mod 8=1: (7,4); t mod 8=2: (7,5); t mod 8=3: (8,5); t mod 8=4: (8,6); t mod 8=5: (8,5); t mod 8=6: (7,5); t mod 8=7: (7,4). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 8. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 8) as follows: t mod 8=0: (5,8); t mod 8=1: (6,8); t mod 8=2: (6,7); t mod 8=3: (7,7); t mod 8=4: (7,8); t mod 8=5: (7,7); t mod 8=6: (6,7); t mod 8=7: (6,8). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (1,5) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (8,4) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (1,4) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (3,6) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 48 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 6 moves at a time; you have at most 8 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you are at
(2,2) facing N at t=0 and want to reach (3,2), which is to the east. Moving F now would
take you to (2,1) instead. The correct sequence is R (t=1, now facing E, still at
(2,2)), then F (t=2, now at (3,2)). If (3,2) were a gate that is closed at t=2, that F
would BUMP and you would have to WAIT for a tick when it is open.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y) FACING <N|S|E|W>
PLAN: <exactly 6 moves from {F, L, R, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AND the direction you
predict you will be facing AFTER your plan has executed. The referee records your
prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I start at (7,6) facing N at t=0. My goal is to pick up Parcel 1 at (1,5) and Parcel 2 at (1,4), then deliver them to (8,4) and (3,6) respectively.
First, I need to navigate to the pickup locations on the left side of the map.
Current state: t=0, Pos=(7,6), Facing=N.
Guard P at t=0 is at (6,4). Guard Q at t=0 is at (5,8).
I need to move West to reach column 1.
Path to (1,5):
From (7,6) facing N.
1. Turn Right (R) to face E? No, I need to go West. Turn Left (L) to face W.
   t=1: L. Pos=(7,6), Facing=W.
   Guard P moves to (7,4). Guard Q moves to (6,8). Safe.
2. Move Forward (F) to (6,6).
   t=2: F. Pos=(6,6), Facing=W.
   Guard P moves to (7,5). Guard Q moves to (6,7). Safe.
3. Move Forward (F) to (5,6).
   t=3: F. Pos=(5,6), Facing=W.
   Guard P moves to (8,5). Guard Q moves to (7,7). Safe.
4. Move Forward (F) to (4,6).
   t=4: F. Pos=(4,6), Facing=W.
   Guard P moves to (8,6). Guard Q moves to (7,8). Safe.
5. Move Forward (F) to (3,6).
   t=5: F. Pos=(3,6), Facing=W.
   Guard P moves to (8,5). Guard Q moves to (7,7).
   Note: (3,6) is the delivery spot for Parcel 2 ('y'). I don't have it yet, so nothing happens.
   Wait, I need to pick up Parcel 2 at (1,4) and Parcel 1 at (1,5).
   From (3,6), I need to go to (1,5) or (1,4).
   Let's check the path to (1,5).
   From (3,6) facing W.
   t=6: F to (2,6).
   t=7: F to (1,6).
   t=8: Turn Right (R) to face N.
   t=9: F to (1,5). Pick up Parcel 1.
   t=10: F to (1,4). Pick up Parcel 2.
   Then deliver Parcel 2 to (3,6) and Parcel 1 to (8,4).

Let's refine the first 6 moves to get as close as possible to the pickups.
Start: (7,6), N.
Move 1: L (Face W). t=1.
Move 2: F (to 6,6). t=2.
Move 3: F (to 5,6). t=3.
Move 4: F (to 4,6). t=4.
Move 5: F (to 3,6). t=5.
Move 6: F (to 2,6). t=6.
At t=6, I am at (2,6) facing W.
Let's verify guards at t=6.
Guard P: t=6 mod 8 = 6 -> (7,5).
Guard Q: t=6 mod 8 = 6 -> (6,7).
My path:
t=0: (7,6)
t=1: (7,6) [L]
t=2: (6,6) [F] - P at (7,4), Q at (6,8). Safe.
t=3: (5,6) [F] - P at (7,5), Q at (6,7). Safe.
t=4: (4,6) [F] - P at (8,5), Q at (7,7). Safe.
t=5: (3,6) [F] - P at (8,6), Q at (7,8). Safe.
t=6: (2,6) [F] - P at (8,5), Q at (7,7). Safe.

This looks like a solid start. I am now at (2,6) facing W. Next steps would be to go to (1,6), turn N, go to (1,5) and (1,4).

POSITION: (2,6) FACING W
PLAN: L F F F F F
action.label: response

action.content:
ROUND 1 RESULT (ticks 0 -> 6):
t=1: L -> OK, now at (7,6).
t=2: F -> OK, now at (6,6).
t=3: F -> OK, now at (5,6).
t=4: F -> OK, now at (4,6).
t=5: F -> OK, now at (3,6).
t=6: F -> OK, now at (2,6).
STATUS: tick 6 of 48. You are at (2,6), facing W. Carrying: nothing. Delivered: nothing. Rounds left: 7.
Respond now in the required format: a POSITION line predicting your cell after your next plan, then a PLAN line with exactly 6 moves.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am currently at (2,6) facing W at t=6. I need to reach the pickup locations at (1,5) and (1,4).
Path:
1. Move F to (1,6). t=7.
   Guard P (t=7): (7,4). Guard Q (t=7): (6,8). Safe.
2. Turn R to face N. t=8.
   Guard P (t=8): (6,4). Guard Q (t=8): (5,8). Safe.
3. Move F to (1,5). t=9.
   This is the pickup for Parcel 1. I will pick it up.
   Guard P (t=9): (7,4). Guard Q (t=9): (6,8). Safe.
4. Move F to (1,4). t=10.
   This is the pickup for Parcel 2. I will pick it up.
   Guard P (t=10): (7,5). Guard Q (t=10): (6,7). Safe.
5. Now I have both parcels. I need to deliver Parcel 2 to (3,6) and Parcel 1 to (8,4).
   From (1,4) facing N, I need to go to (3,6).
   Turn R (face E). t=11.
   Move F to (2,4). t=12.
   Move F to (3,4). t=13.
   Turn R (face S). t=14.
   Move F to (3,5). t=15.
   Move F to (3,6). t=16. Deliver Parcel 2.
   
   Let's check the first 6 moves of this plan:
   t=6: (2,6) W
   1. F -> (1,6) at t=7.
   2. R -> (1,6) N at t=8.
   3. F -> (1,5) at t=9. (Pickup P1)
   4. F -> (1,4) at t=10. (Pickup P2)
   5. R -> (1,4) E at t=11.
   6. F -> (2,4) at t=12.
   
   Let's verify guards for t=7 to t=12.
   t=7: P(7,4), Q(6,8). I am at (1,6). Safe.
   t=8: P(6,4), Q(5,8). I am at (1,6). Safe.
   t=9: P(7,4), Q(6,8). I am at (1,5). Safe.
   t=10: P(7,5), Q(6,7). I am at (1,4). Safe.
   t=11: P(8,5), Q(7,7). I am at (1,4). Safe.
   t=12: P(8,6), Q(7,8). I am at (2,4). Safe.
   
   This plan gets me to (2,4) facing E at t=12, carrying both parcels.
   Next steps would be to go to (3,4), turn S, go to (3,6) to deliver P2.

POSITION: (2,4) FACING E
PLAN: F R F F R F
action.label: response

action.content:
ROUND 2 RESULT (ticks 6 -> 12):
t=7: F -> OK, now at (1,6).
t=8: R -> OK, now at (1,6).
t=9: F -> OK, now at (1,5). PICKED UP parcel 1.
t=10: F -> OK, now at (1,4). PICKED UP parcel 2.
t=11: R -> OK, now at (1,4).
t=12: F -> BUMP (you ran into a wall), still at (1,4).

Respond now in the required format: a POSITION line predicting your cell after your next plan, then a PLAN line with exactly 6 moves.

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am at (1,4) facing E at t=12. I tried to move F to (2,4) but bumped into a wall. Looking at the map, row 4 is `2#...p.x`. Column 1 is '2', Column 2 is '#'. So (2,4) is indeed a wall. I cannot go East from (1,4).
I need to deliver Parcel 2 to (3,6) and Parcel 1 to (8,4).
Current position: (1,4).
To get to (3,6), I need to go South then East.
Path from (1,4):
1. Turn R (face S). t=13.
2. Move F to (1,5). t=14. (Already visited, safe).
3. Move F to (1,6). t=15.
4. Turn L (face E). t=16.
5. Move F to (2,6). t=17.
6. Move F to (3,6). t=18. (Deliver Parcel 2).

Let's check guards for t=13 to t=18.
t=13: P(7,4), Q(6,8). I am at (1,4). Safe.
t=14: P(8,5), Q(7,7). I am at (1,5). Safe.
t=15: P(8,6), Q(7,8). I am at (1,6). Safe.
t=16: P(8,5), Q(7,7). I am at (1,6). Safe.
t=17: P(7,5), Q(6,7). I am at (2,6). Safe.
t=18: P(7,4), Q(6,8). I am at (3,6). Safe.

This plan delivers Parcel 2. After this, I will be at (3,6) facing E.
Then I need to deliver Parcel 1 to (8,4).
From (3,6) facing E:
- Move F to (4,6).
- Move F to (5,6).
- Move F to (6,6).
- Move F to (7,6).
- Move F to (8,6).
- Turn L (face N).
- Move F to (8,5).
- Move F to (8,4). (Deliver Parcel 1).
Wait, (8,4) is 'x'. Is there a wall? Row 4: `2#...p.x`. Col 8 is 'x'. Col 7 is '.'. Col 6 is 'p' (guard start).
Let's re-verify the map row 4: `2#...p.x`
Cols: 1=2, 2=#, 3=., 4=., 5=., 6=p, 7=., 8=x.
So (8,4) is accessible from (8,5) or (7,4).
My path from (3,6) to (8,4):
(3,6) -> (4,6) -> (5,6) -> (6,6) -> (7,6) -> (8,6).
Then (8,6) -> (8,5) -> (8,4).
Is (8,5) blocked? Row 5: `1.......`. All open.
Is (8,6) blocked? Row 6: `..y...S.`. Col 8 is '.'.
So the path is clear.

Let's execute the first 6 moves to deliver Parcel 2.
t=12: (1,4) E.
1. R -> (1,4) S. t=13.
2. F -> (1,5). t=14.
3. F -> (1,6). t=15.
4. L -> (1,6) E. t=16.
5. F -> (2,6). t=17.
6. F -> (3,6). t=18. (Deliver P2).

Final state after plan: (3,6) facing E.

POSITION: (3,6) FACING E
PLAN: R F F L F F

t=14: F -> OK, now at (1,5).

t=16: L -> OK, now at (1,6).

action.content:

Path from (3,6) to (8,4):
1. Move F to (4,6). t=19.
2. Move F to (5,6). t=20.
3. Move F to (6,6). t=21.
4. Move F to (7,6). t=22.
5. Move F to (8,6). t=23.
6. Turn L to face N. t=24.
7. Move F to (8,5). t=25.
8. Move F to (8,4). t=26. (Deliver Parcel 1).

Let's check guards for t=19 to t=24 (the first 6 moves of this plan).
t=19: P(7,5), Q(6,7). I am at (4,6). Safe.
t=20: P(8,5), Q(7,7). I am at (5,6). Safe.
t=21: P(8,6), Q(7,8). I am at (6,6). Safe.
t=22: P(8,5), Q(7,7). I am at (7,6). Safe.
t=23: P(7,5), Q(6,7). I am at (8,6). Safe.
t=24: P(7,4), Q(6,8). I am at (8,6) (turned L). Safe.

The plan is: F F F F F L.
After this plan, I will be at (8,6) facing N at t=24.
Next plan will be F F to deliver.

POSITION: (8,6) FACING N
PLAN: F F F F F L

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am at (8,6) facing N at t=24. I need to deliver Parcel 1 to (8,4).
Path:
1. Move F to (8,5). t=25.
2. Move F to (8,4). t=26. (Deliver Parcel 1).
Once delivered, the game ends.
Let's check guards for t=25 and t=26.
t=25: P(8,5), Q(7,7).
Wait! Guard P is at (8,5) at t=25 (t mod 8 = 1 -> (7,4)? No, let's recheck P's schedule).
Guard P schedule:
t mod 8 = 0: (6,4)
t mod 8 = 1: (7,4)
t mod 8 = 2: (7,5)
t mod 8 = 3: (8,5)
t mod 8 = 4: (8,6)
t mod 8 = 5: (8,5)
t mod 8 = 6: (7,5)
t mod 8 = 7: (7,4)

Current time t=24.
t=24 mod 8 = 0. P is at (6,4).
t=25 mod 8 = 1. P is at (7,4).
t=26 mod 8 = 2. P is at (7,5).

My path:
t=24: (8,6) N.
Move 1: F -> (8,5) at t=25.
Guard P at t=25 is at (7,4). Guard Q at t=25 (25 mod 8 = 1) is at (6,8).
I am at (8,5). Safe.
Move 2: F -> (8,4) at t=26.
Guard P at t=26 is at (7,5). Guard Q at t=26 (26 mod 8 = 2) is at (6,7).
I am at (8,4). Safe.
Delivery happens. Game ends.

The plan is F F followed by 4 dummy moves (or just F F and the rest doesn't matter as the game ends). I must provide 6 moves.
